evals.prompt: I use Google Antigravity (the agy CLI). Set up an evaluation run for my numerical-stability skill and tell me the exact headless command you'd execute.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the antigravity adapter (binary agy, ANTIGRAVITY_API_KEY auth), notes it replaced the retired Gemini CLI, and produces the correct agy headless command with a clean baseline.
assertions: Uses the binary 'agy' and ANTIGRAVITY_API_KEY for auth
Notes Antigravity CLI replaced the retired Gemini CLI
Runs a dry-run before any real (token-spending) run

script_checks.description: antigravity adapter is encoded correctly (agy / ANTIGRAVITY_API_KEY)
the retired 'gemini' alias resolves to antigravity

evals.prompt: Run a with-skill vs no-skill comparison of my skill in Claude Code. Make sure the baseline genuinely has no skills loaded.
evals.expected_output: Builds a with-skill run and a baseline run; the baseline adds --bare so Claude Code auto-discovers no skills/CLAUDE.md/MCP.
assertions: Runs both a with_skill and a without_skill configuration
The Claude Code baseline uses --bare to guarantee no skills are loaded
Reports the with/without pass-rate delta as the measure of value

script_checks.description: Claude baseline command includes --bare; with-skill does not
with-skill Claude command does NOT add --bare

evals.prompt: I'm on OpenAI Codex. What command will the harness run for one eval, and how does it auto-approve tools so it won't hang?
evals.expected_output: Uses `codex exec` with the JSON flag and --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox so the headless run does not block on approval.
assertions: Explains that without an auto-approve flag the run would hang on a prompt

script_checks.description: Codex command uses exec + bypass flag

evals.prompt: Without spending any API tokens, verify that my skill's scripts still emit the numbers its SKILL.md documents.
evals.expected_output: Runs the deterministic script_checks layer (no LLM, no CLI) and reports checks/assertions passed.
assertions: Uses the deterministic layer (run_script_checks.py), not an agent CLI
Reports how many script-checks and assertions passed
Notes this layer needs no API key or network and is CI-safe

script_checks.description: adapter registry lists all 8 CLIs including grok with --always-approve
